questions.question: The Agent SDK (TS/Python) just spawns the `claude` CLI binary and talks NDJSON on stdio. We can do the same directly from Lua with `vim.uv.spawn()` — zero extra dependencies, all CC features (skills, hooks, MCP, auth) work automatically. Does this approach work for you, or did you have a specific reason for wanting the Agent SDK layer?

options.label: CLI direct (Recommended)
options.description: Spawn `claude` binary from Lua via vim.uv.spawn(). Zero deps beyond what you have. Same NDJSON protocol the SDK uses internally.

options.label: Agent SDK (TS sidecar)
options.description: Run a Node.js process using @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, communicate with Neovim via RPC. Adds Node.js dependency but gives typed SDK helpers.

options.label: Agent SDK (Python sidecar)
options.description: Run a Python process using claude-agent-sdk, communicate with Neovim via RPC. Adds Python dependency.

questions.question: How should the two buffers be arranged by default?

options.label: Horizontal split
options.description: Output on top, prompt on bottom (like a chat). Both full-width.
options.preview:
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  Output Buffer (scrollable)  │
│  ── User ──────────────────  │
│  Fix the bug in auth.ts...   │
│  ── Agent ─────────────────  │
│  I'll investigate...         │
│    ▶ Read src/auth.ts        │
│    ▶ Edit src/auth.ts        │
│  Fixed. The expiry was...    │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│  Prompt Buffer (editable)    │
│  |                           │
└──────────────────────────────┘

options.label: Vertical split
options.description: Output on left, prompt on right. Side-by-side.

options.label: Tab-based
options.description: Output and prompt in the same buffer area, switch via tab/command. Maximizes screen real estate for each.

questions.question: For the MVP, how should permissions be handled when Claude wants to use a restricted tool?

options.label: Auto-approve all
options.description: Pass --permission-mode auto. No prompts. Fastest iteration, relies on your existing CC permissions config for safety rails.

options.label: Inline vim.ui.select
options.description: Show a native Neovim picker (Allow/Deny/Always) when a permission request arrives. Interrupts flow but gives control.

options.label: Configurable default
options.description: Default to auto-approve for MVP, add vim.ui.select prompt as a Phase 3 feature. Config option to switch between them.

content.content:
User has answered your questions: "The Agent SDK (TS/Python) just spawns the `claude` CLI binary and talks NDJSON on stdio. We can do the same directly from Lua with `vim.uv.spawn()` — zero extra dependencies, all CC features (skills, hooks, MCP, auth) work automatically. Does this approach work for you, or did you have a specific reason for wanting the Agent SDK layer?"="CLI direct (Recommended)", "How should the two buffers be arranged by default?"="Horizontal split" selected preview:

│  |                           │
└──────────────────────────────┘, "For the MVP, how should permissions be handled when Claude wants to use a restricted tool?"="Inline vim.ui.select". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
